response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack web developer with strong TypeScript/Node.js and DLang credentials, but the profile misses nearly every core requirement for this embedded networking security role: no evidence of Linux kernel investigation or modification, no L2/L3 networking or switch experience, no security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption), and critically no Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard requirement. C experience is limited to a brief six-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing culture, and open-source contributions
Some C/C++ and Linux exposure (LabWindows/CVI components, audio server thesis project)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, a mandatory requirement for the role
No demonstrated experience with Linux kernel internals, L2/L3 networking, network switches, or security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X)
C proficiency appears shallow and dated; career trajectory is web/full-stack, not embedded firmware

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or kernel experience
Strong general engineering and testing background
